title: Ownership closure audit before Python reduction

go_sidecar.state: existing service may be active on 127.0.0.1:5002
go_sidecar.ownership_status: not selected by tested Caddy routes in 23.9

go_implemented_candidates.owner_status: implemented candidate; live ownership depends on explicit routing gate
go_implemented_candidates.runtime_guard: default Go runtime keeps SQLite query_only=true unless a local write flag is enabled
go_implemented_candidates.live_status_after_23_9: Python remains owner for tested public routes

go_implemented_candidates.runtime_guard: requires --enable-tag-write and/or --enable-category-write; disables query_only only for copied local DB smoke
go_implemented_candidates.live_status_after_23_9: not live-routed; production writes remain Python-owned

go_implemented_candidates.runtime_guard: requires --enable-attachment-text-read and explicit data-dir; raw=true/binary/send_file remain blocked
go_implemented_candidates.live_status_after_23_9: not live-routed; production attachment reads remain Python-owned

go_implemented_candidates.runtime_guard: local smoke artifact serves bundled dist against copied data
go_implemented_candidates.live_status_after_23_9: Pi production SPA serving remains Python/Flask plus existing Caddy path

retained_python_owned_surfaces.retention_reason: 23.9 live verification kept Python owner and did not expand Go routing
multi-table writes, history, cascade/file cleanup, nested matcher risk, and rollback scope remain Python-owned
Go update candidates are local/copied-DB only; create/delete/merge/cascade behavior remains Python-owned
filesystem mutation, remote fetch, thumbnails, send_file, backup/restore, import/export, and cleanup compensation remain Python-owned
maintenance, local config, process control, logs, backups, and status endpoints remain Python-owned and local/trusted-network only
23.7 selected retained Python migrations; Go only reads Schema_Meta for health/status
Go embedded SPA is local artifact capability only; Pi production serving path was not changed in 23.9

deprecated_or_removed_surfaces.retention_reason: not part of Phase 23 Python reduction
must remain until 23.10.2+ explicitly decides every critical retained surface

python_removal_readiness.reason: Critical live writes, files, migrations, system/server maintenance, frontend/static serving, and rollback ownership still have retained Python decisions rather than Go implementations.
minimum_before_any_removal: Every retained Python-owned critical surface has either a verified Go implementation or a signed retained-Python release strategy.
Live route ownership matrix is verified on Pi with headers, service state, Caddy validation, migration status, and rollback evidence.
Local packaged artifact and Pi deployment both pass full smoke, full pytest, frontend build, browser/API checks, backup, and rollback drill.
Docs, scripts, requirements, service units, and user-facing deployment instructions agree on the chosen runtime path.
blocked_actions: Remove Python from normal runtime path
Point frontend default API target to Go
Promote Go writes/files/migrations to live ownership
Remove Pillow or Python thumbnail path
Expose Prism directly to public internet

allowed_next_step.title: Python removal decision
allowed_next_step.expected_decision: retain Python unless every critical surface has a Go implementation or explicit retained-Python release strategy
allowed_next_step.scope: Decide whether Python can be removed from the normal path. This gate may also close as retained-Python if the ownership audit shows critical gaps.

parallel_deferred_track.title: Go WebP encoder dependency decision
parallel_deferred_track.scope: Thumbnail ownership remains separate; Pillow removal is not authorized by this audit.
